quality.rationale: 基于作用量变分原理推导测地线方程，需串联欧拉-拉格朗日方程、度规协变导数与克里斯托费尔符号定义，逻辑严密且适用于任意伪黎曼流形

origin.question: Derive the geodesic equation from the definition of a geodesic as a curve of extremal length.
origin.solution: Solution 7.10. The integral for length is$$\int \mathrm {d} \mathbf {s} = \int \left(- \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \beta} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {x}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm {d} s} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {x}^{\beta}}{\mathrm {d} s}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathrm {d} \mathbf {s}.$$To extremize length take $\delta \int \mathrm{d}s = 0$ . According to the Euler-Lagrange equations$$\frac {\mathrm {d}}{\mathrm {d} \mathrm {s}} \left(\mathrm {g}_{\alpha \beta} \mathrm {u}^{\alpha}\right) = \frac {1}{2} \mathrm {g}_{\alpha \gamma , \beta} \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\gamma} \tag {1}$$where $\left(-\mathbf{g}_{\alpha \beta}\frac{\mathrm{d}\mathbf{x}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm{d}s}\frac{\mathrm{d}\mathbf{x}^{\beta}}{\mathrm{d}s}\right) = 1$ and $\mathbf{u}^{\alpha}\equiv \frac{\mathrm{d}\mathbf{x}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm{d}s}$ have been used. Now since$$\frac {\mathrm {d}}{\mathrm {d} s} \left(\mathbf {g}_{\alpha \beta} \mathbf {u}^{\alpha}\right) = \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \beta} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {u}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm {d} s} + \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {x}^{\gamma}}{\mathrm {d} s} \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \beta , \gamma} \mathbf {u}^{\alpha} = \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \beta} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {u}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm {d} s} + \mathbf {u}^{\gamma} \mathbf {u}^{\alpha} \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \beta , \gamma}$$Equation (1) becomes$$\mathrm {g}_{\alpha \beta} \frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathrm {x}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm {d s}^{2}} + \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\gamma} \left(\mathrm {g}_{\alpha \beta , \gamma} - \frac {1}{2} \mathrm {g}_{\alpha \gamma , \beta}\right) = 0. \tag {2}$$Now, use$$\mathsf {u}^{\alpha} \mathsf {u}^{\gamma} \mathsf {g}_{\alpha \beta , \gamma} = \mathsf {u}^{\alpha} \mathsf {u}^{\gamma} \cdot \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathsf {g}_{\alpha \beta , \gamma} + \mathsf {g}_{\gamma \beta , a}\right)$$and multiply Equation (2) by $g^{\beta \tau}$ to obtain$$\begin{array}{l} \frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathbf {x}^{\tau}}{\mathrm {d s}^{2}} + \frac {1}{2} \mathrm {g}^{\beta \tau} \left(\mathrm {g}_{\alpha \beta , \gamma} + \mathrm {g}_{\gamma \beta , \alpha} - \mathrm {g}_{\alpha \gamma , \beta}\right) \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\gamma} \tag {3} \\= \frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathbf {x}^{\tau}}{\mathrm {d s}^{2}} + \Gamma_{\alpha \gamma}^{\tau} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {x}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm {d s}} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {x}^{\gamma}}{\mathrm {d s}} = 0. \\\end {array}$$
